### G. Business / Governance

**27. Self-approval of expense reimbursements without co-member oversight.** The NovaBridge expense reports (PROD-007, Section 1) state that under Section 5.3 of the Operating Agreement, a Managing Member holding a majority interest (60% or greater) approves his own expense reimbursements "without requirement of separate board or member approval," and that Jared Ostrowski's co-member review is "N/A — not required." Respondent thus controls his own reimbursement authority, creating a vehicle to convert company funds to personal use disguised as business expenses (and a potential source of undocumented income/distributions). *Sources: PROD-007 §1, §5.*

**28. The Operating Agreement (referenced in PROD-007) was not produced.** Section 5.3 of the NovaBridge Operating Agreement is cited and relied upon in the expense reports but the Operating Agreement itself was not produced; the cover letter states Request Nos. 16–22 (which include the Operating Agreement and entity-level records) remain outstanding. The agreement's transfer restrictions, buyout provisions, and compensation/distribution provisions are directly relevant to valuation and dissipation. *Sources: PROD-007 §1; Cover letter (RFP Nos. 16–22).*

**29. Expense reimbursements do not reconcile to bank deposits.** The expense reports (PROD-007) show Q1 $3,373 (approved 4/8) and Q2 $9,289 (approved 7/3), totaling $12,662. The FCB checking (PROD-004) shows only two "NovaBridge Solutions LLC Expense Reimb." deposits: $3,400 (4/10) and $2,150 (5/20), totaling $5,550 — leaving approximately **$7,112** of reported reimbursements unaccounted for in the produced bank records. Additionally, the 4/10 deposit ($3,400) does not match the Q1 report total ($3,373), a $27 discrepancy. *Sources: PROD-007 §4; PROD-004 (4/10 and 5/20 reimbursement deposits).*

**30. Same-day, near-identical "personal devices" purchases on the personal card and as a company reimbursement.** On **May 17, 2024**, the credit card (PROD-006) shows a $2,149.50 charge at "Pinnacle Electronics — Personal Devices," and the Q2 expense report (PROD-007) shows a $2,150.00 charge on the same date at "TechVault Solutions" for "Technology Equipment — Personal Devices" (tablet, smartphone, accessories). The same date, near-identical amount, and identical "personal devices" description suggest possible double payment or commingling of personal and business purchases; the underlying receipts should be examined. *Sources: PROD-006 May 2024 (5/17 Pinnacle Electronics); PROD-007 §3 (5/17 TechVault).*

**31. Business interest not valued.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶37) states the value of Respondent's 60% NovaBridge interest "has not been formally appraised" and reserves the right to engage a valuator. The K-1 (PROD-002) reports an ending tax-basis capital account of $675,800 and recourse liabilities of $36,400, and the entity summary shows $322,000 retained at the entity level (60% share $193,200). No valuation has been provided. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶37; PROD-002 Schedule K-1 (capital account, liabilities) and entity summary.*

### H. Production / Internal Inconsistencies

**32. Conflicting separation dates.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶5) states the parties "effectively separated on or about June 1, 2024." The cover letter's privilege log describes withheld communications "following the parties' separation on August 1, 2024" (PRIV-001) and "post-separation August 1, 2024" (PRIV-002). The date of separation is a controlling date for identifying the marital estate and for characterizing the $85,000 wire (Item 10) and other transfers; the discrepancy must be resolved. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶5; Cover letter (PRIV-001, PRIV-002).*

**33. Conflicting filing date for the Sworn Financial Declaration.** The cover letter states the Declaration was "filed with the Court on or about October 4, 2024," while the Declaration itself states "Filed: September 20, 2024" and is notarized and certified as served on September 20, 2024. *Sources: Cover letter (PROD-001); PROD-001 (Filed line, notarization, certificate of service).*

**34. Case-number and court-name error on the FCB production stamp.** The FCB statements (PROD-004) bear a production stamp reading "Case No. 2024-DR-**00847**, Circuit Court of **Columbia, Fairview County**." The correct caption (cover letter; PROD-001; PROD-007) is Case No. 2024-DR-**01847**, Circuit Court of **Fairview County, Columbia**. The bank production misstates both the case number and the court name. *Sources: PROD-004 (production stamp); Cover letter; PROD-001; PROD-007.*

**35. Inconsistent Bates-numbering schemes.** The tax-return excerpts use the prefix "DK-PROD-" (DK-PROD-0045 – 0058), while the Crestline, FCB, and NovaBridge productions use "KWAN_PROD_" (e.g., KWAN_PROD_00142, 0401, 00187). The mixed prefixes complicate indexing and citation. *Sources: PROD-002 (DK-PROD-); PROD-003, PROD-004, PROD-007 (KWAN_PROD_).*

**36. Missing Bates ranges on two productions.** The credit-card statements (PROD-006) and the Elm Street mortgage statements (PROD-005) do not bear or state any Bates-stamp range, unlike the other five sets. *Sources: PROD-005; PROD-006 (no Bates range identified).*

**37. Undated cover letter.** The transmittal email's "Date:" field is blank; the production cover letter is undated. *Source: Cover letter (Date field).*

**38. Conflicting contact information for Respondent's counsel.** The Declaration (PROD-001, prepared-by block) lists Raymond Fossett at telephone (571) 555-0184, fax (571) 555-0185, email rfossett@**hfylaw**.com. The cover letter signature block lists the same attorney at telephone (571) 555-4200, fax (571) 555-4201, email rfossett@**hfy-law**.com. The telephone, fax, and email domain all differ. *Sources: PROD-001 (prepared-by block); Cover letter (signature block).*

**39. Brokerage account tax ID does not match the account holder's SSN.** The Crestline statements (PROD-003) list the account Tax ID as ●●●-●●-**8341**. Respondent's SSN, per the tax return (PROD-002), ends in **4718**; the suffix 8341 instead matches the dependent child Ethan Kwan's SSN (XXX-XX-8341). The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶29) represents the account is "held in Respondent's name alone." The mismatch should be reconciled (possible titling/custodial issue or statement error). *Sources: PROD-003 (account information, all statements); PROD-002 (SSNs for Darren and Ethan Kwan); PROD-001 ¶29.*

**40. Brokerage and bank mailing addresses remained the marital residence through August 2024.** Although the Declaration (PROD-001, ¶2) states Respondent moved to 2200 Harbor Point Drive on or about June 1, 2024, the Crestline statements (PROD-003) and FCB statements (PROD-004) continue to show the mailing address as 418 Birchwood Lane through August 1, 2024 (only the credit card, PROD-006, shows an address change to Harbor Point effective the July cycle). The continued use of the marital-residence address for investment and bank mail should be confirmed as merely administrative. *Sources: PROD-003 (all statements); PROD-004 (all statements); PROD-006 (July–Aug. address change); PROD-001 ¶2.*

### I. Tax-Return Technical Items

**41. K-1 interest income may not be reflected on Form 1040.** The Schedule K-1 (PROD-002) reports $1,240 of interest income in Box 5 (pass-through from NovaBridge). Form 1040 Line 2b reports total taxable interest of $1,840, which Schedule B attributes entirely to "accounts at Fairview Community Bank." The K-1 Box 5 interest does not appear to be included in Line 2b, suggesting possible underreported interest income of $1,240 (or an incomplete Schedule B summary). *Sources: PROD-002 Schedule K-1 Box 5; Form 1040 Line 2b; Schedule B summary.*

**42. QBI deduction appears to be zero despite Form 8995 being filed.** The tax computation (PROD-002) shows taxable income (Line 15, $564,240) equal to AGI ($612,960) less itemized deductions ($48,720) only — i.e., no Qualified Business Income deduction appears on Line 13, even though Form 8995 was filed and NovaBridge generated $367,200 of QBI with substantial W-2 wages. A nonzero QBI deduction might be expected at these facts; Form 8995 was not produced. *Sources: PROD-002 Form 1040 Lines 11–15; Schedule K-1 Box 1; "Additional Schedules" summary (Form 8995 referenced but not excerpted).*

**43. Only excerpts of the 47-page return were produced.** The tax-return cover sheet (PROD-002) states the complete return comprises 47 pages and that Schedule A, Schedule B (detail), Schedule D, Schedule SE, and Form 8995 were filed but are "not produced in these excerpts" (only summarized). The full return and all schedules should be obtained. *Sources: PROD-002 cover sheet and "Additional Schedules" summary.*

### J. Production Completeness Gaps

**44. Joint account statements (FCB 4821 and 4822) not produced.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶¶25–26) discloses a joint checking account (ending 4821, $12,340) and joint savings account (ending 4822, $28,750) held jointly with Petitioner. No statements for either joint account were produced. As a joint account holder, Respondent has possession, custody, or control of these records; the cover letter's objection to producing "accounts held solely by Petitioner" does not extend to joint accounts. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶¶25–26, 32; Cover letter (general reservations); PROD-004 (individual accounts only).*

**45. 401(k) account statement (Pinnacle Retirement, acct. 3310) not produced.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶30) reports a 401(k) balance of $412,800. No 401(k) statement was produced, and the FCB records (PROD-004) show ongoing contributions including the anomalous $14,644.60 transfer on 8/1/2024 (Item 12). *Sources: PROD-001 ¶30; PROD-004 (Pinnacle 401(k) ACH entries).*

**46. Marital-residence mortgage statement not produced.** No statement for the Birchwood Lane mortgage (the Declaration's largest disclosed liability, $218,400) was produced; only the Elm Street mortgage was produced (Item 9). *Sources: PROD-001 ¶¶20–22, 44; Cover letter (PROD-005 = Elm Street only).*

**47. Auto-loan documentation not produced.** No statement or loan document for the BMW auto loan ($22,100, FCB Auto Finance) was produced, and the $425/month payment is not visible in the produced checking account (Item 26). *Sources: PROD-001 ¶45; PROD-004.*

**48. Entity-level NovaBridge records not produced (RFP Nos. 16–22).** The cover letter confirms the NovaBridge Operating Agreement, entity-level financial records, Form 1065, and corporate records were not produced and will be "supplemented." These are essential to value the 60% interest, to test the $174,000 distribution against entity cash flow, and to assess whether additional distributions or disguised compensation occurred. *Sources: Cover letter (RFP Nos. 16–22); PROD-002 entity summary; PROD-007 §1.*

**49. Formal privilege log not yet produced.** The cover letter withholds PRIV-001 through PRIV-004 on attorney-client/work-product grounds but states a formal privilege log "in full compliance with the applicable rules is forthcoming … within ten (10) business days." The log had not been served as of this production. *Source: Cover letter (Privilege Log section).*

**50. Petitioner's reciprocal production outstanding.** The cover letter notes that Respondent's First Request for Production served on Petitioner (Sept. 18, 2024) "remains outstanding." Petitioner's production (including her 403(b) statements, her FCB joint-account access, and her employment records) should be pursued. *Source: Cover letter (final paragraph).*

---

## V. Recommended Follow-Up Discovery

The following discovery steps are recommended, keyed to the issues above.

### A. Document demands / meet-and-confer on the existing production

1. **Demand supplementation and correction of the Sworn Financial Declaration (PROD-001)** to (i) report K-1 ordinary business income ($367,200) rather than distributions ($174,000); (ii) add the omitted interest, dividend, capital-gain, and rental income; (iii) add the 1035 Elm Street property and its mortgage and rental income; (iv) add the $9,600 vehicle allowance; (v) correct the credit-card creditor to Fairview Community Bank and the payment pattern; (vi) restate all account balances as of the June 1, 2024 separation date (or such other date as the Court fixes); and (vii) disclose the Harborstone Financial Group account (ending 2291). Consider a motion to compel and for sanctions if not corrected.

2. **Meet and confer on the separation date** (Item 32). The June 1, 2024 vs. August 1, 2024 conflict must be resolved because it controls characterization of the $85,000 wire and other transfers. Request a sworn statement of the factual basis for each date.

3. **Demand production of the joint accounts** (FCB 4821 and 4822) (Item 44). Respondent is a joint account holder and the "solely Petitioner's accounts" objection does not apply.

4. **Demand the full 47-page 2023 tax return** with all schedules (A, B detail, D, SE, Form 8995) and the corresponding 2022 return (Items 41–43). Also demand 2024 quarterly estimated-tax payment records (the $10,550 Q1 estimate paid from Crestline on 4/15/2024).

5. **Demand the formal privilege log** (Item 49) if not served within the represented ten business days; scrutinize PRIV-003 ("Preliminary analysis of marital estate," Aug. 20, 2024) for any portion not protected (e.g., factual financial data vs. legal advice).

### B. Targeted document requests / subpoenas to third parties

6. **Subpoena Harborstone Financial Group** for all records of account ending 2291 (and any other accounts in Respondent's name or controlled by him) from January 2023 to present (Items 10, 18). This is the single most important follow-up: it will show where the $85,000 went and may reveal the funding source for the credit card and other payments.

7. **Subpoena Fairview Community Bank** for (i) all accounts in Darren Kwan's name (including any second Visa card and the auto-loan account) and all joint accounts (4821, 4822) from January 2023 to present; and (ii) the auto-loan (FCB Auto Finance) note and payment history (Items 18, 19, 26, 44, 47).

8. **Subpoena Pinnacle Retirement Services** for the 401(k) account (ending 3310) statements and transaction history from January 2023 to present, including loan/withdrawal records and the August 1, 2024 $14,644.60 transaction (Items 12, 45).

9. **Subpoena Meridian Home Lending** for the **418 Birchwood Lane** mortgage note, payment history, and current payoff (Item 46), in addition to the Elm Street loan already produced.

10. **Subpoena Crestline Wealth Advisors** for the complete account history (account 9956) from account inception, all wire/transfer records, the advisory agreement, and confirmation of the account's tax ID/registration (Items 10, 39, 40).

11. **Subpoena NovaBridge Solutions LLC** (and serve on Jared Ostrowski) for the Operating Agreement (with all amendments), Form 1065 and full partnership return for 2022 and 2023, entity-level financial statements, bank statements, the general ledger, member capital accounts, distribution registers, and all expense-reimbursement records for both members (Items 27, 28, 29, 31, 48).

12. **Serve a discovery request / deposition notice on Respondent** requiring identification of "Kiera M." and the purpose of the $14,600 in Venmo transfers, and production of all Venmo/Zelle/CashApp and peer-transfer records (Item 11).

### C. Depositions and expert work

13. **Depose Darren Kwan** on the inconsistencies in Items 1–15, 22–30, and 32–33, with the produced documents marked as exhibits; focus on the Elm Street omission, the $85,000 wire, the "Kiera M." transfers, the self-approved expense reimbursements, the separation date, and the income/distribution distinction.

14. **Depose Jared Ostrowski** (co-member, 40%) on NovaBridge distributions, compensation, the Operating Agreement, expense-reimbursement practices, and any knowledge of Respondent's personal financial conduct (Items 27–31, 48).

15. **Retain a forensic accountant** to (i) reconstruct Respondent's actual 2023 and 2024 income from the bank, brokerage, and tax records; (ii) trace the $85,000 wire and the "Kiera M." transfers; (iii) reconcile the credit-card and expense-reimbursement flows; and (iv) value the 60% NovaBridge interest (Items 1, 2, 10, 11, 18, 29, 31).

16. **Obtain a formal appraisal** of the marital residence (418 Birchwood Lane) and the Elm Street condominium (1035 Elm Street, Unit C), and a business valuation of NovaBridge (Items 7, 9, 31).

### D. Motion practice

17. **Consider a motion for a preliminary injunction / freezing order** or an accounting under the applicable Columbia family-code provisions to prevent further dissipation, given the $85,000 post-separation wire, the escalating credit-card spending, and the self-approved reimbursement authority (Items 10, 17, 22–23, 27).

18. **Consider a motion to compel** the RFP Nos. 16–22 productions (Operating Agreement and entity records) on an expedited schedule, and to compel the joint-account and 401(k) statements (Items 44–48).

19. **Preserve a record for sanctions / adverse inference** should Respondent fail to correct the Declaration's material misstatements and omissions identified in Items 1–9, 13–15.

---

## VI. Conclusion

The production, while facially responsive to seven categories of requests, is internally contradictory and materially incomplete. Respondent's own tax return (PROD-002) and bank/brokerage records (PROD-003, PROD-004, PROD-005) directly refute core representations in his Sworn Financial Declaration (PROD-001): the Declaration omits a separately titled rental property and its mortgage and income; understates 2023 income by roughly $213,000 by reporting K-1 distributions as income and ignoring investment and rental income; reports post-separation (August 1, 2024) account balances as separation-date values, masking an $85,000 wire to an undisclosed Harborstone Financial Group account; and understates monthly expenses and liabilities. The records further reveal recurring transfers to an unidentified "Kiera M." ($14,600), a self-approved business-expense reimbursement structure, and a non-reconciling credit-card payment stream that implies an undisclosed funding account. Immediate follow-up discovery — particularly a subpoena to Harborstone Financial Group, production of the joint and 401(k) accounts, the NovaBridge Operating Agreement and entity records, and a deposition of Respondent — is warranted, together with a demand that the Sworn Financial Declaration be corrected under oath.

*Prepared for internal use. This memorandum is attorney work product and contains privileged and confidential information.*"}

{"file_path": "memo_draft.md", "content": "# INTERNAL MEMORANDUM — ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT / PRIVILEGED & CONFIDENTIAL

**TO:** Sarah Whitfield, Esq.; Jordan Reeves
**FROM:** [Associate], Calloway & Birch LLP
**DATE:** [Date of review]
**RE:** *Torres-Kwan v. Kwan*, Case No. 2024-DR-01847 (Cir. Ct. Fairview County, Columbia; Hon. Patricia Yoon-Callahan) — Review of Respondent Darren Kwan's Discovery Production (PROD-001 through PROD-007) and Recommended Follow-Up Discovery

---

## I. Introduction and Scope

This memorandum catalogs, summarizes, and analyzes the document production served by Respondent Darren Kwan ("Respondent") through counsel Raymond Fossett (Hargrove Fossett & Yates LLP) in response to Petitioner Melinda Torres-Kwan's ("Petitioner") First Request for Production of Documents, Nos. 1–22. The production consists of a transmittal email (cover letter), seven (7) document sets designated PROD-001 through PROD-007, and a summary privilege log identifying four (4) withheld items (PRIV-001 through PRIV-004).

The production is reviewed below in four parts: (A) an exhibit catalog; (B) a summary of the financial figures reported; (C) a detailed, itemized list of inconsistencies, omissions, and red flags identified by cross-referencing the produced documents against each other; and (D) recommended follow-up discovery. **The central finding is that Respondent's Sworn Financial Declaration (PROD-001) materially understates income, omits a separately titled investment real-property asset and its associated mortgage and rental income, and reports account balances as of August 1, 2024 while representing them as separation-date (June 1, 2024) values — thereby masking an $85,000 post-separation wire transfer to an undisclosed account at Harborstone Financial Group.** The produced records also reveal a pattern of large, recurring transfers to an unidentified third party ("Kiera M."), escalating personal spending inconsistent with the declared expense schedule, and a business-expense reimbursement structure that permits Respondent to self-approve his own reimbursements without co-member oversight.

---

## II. Exhibit Catalog

Schedule E (Parts I & II), Schedule K-1, W-2s, entity summary | DK-PROD-0045 – DK-PROD-0058 | Tax year 2023; signed Apr. 14, 2024 | Archer & Linden CPAs / D. | PROD-006 | Platinum Visa credit card statements (Fairview Community Bank, acct. ending 8316) | Not stated | May–Aug. 2024 billing periods | Fairview Community Bank | 6, 7, 11 | Monthly charges; paid in full monthly; address change to Harbor Point July |
| PROD-007 | NovaBridge Solutions LLC expense reimbursement reports, Darren Kwan, Q1–Q2 2024 | KWAN_PROD_00187 – KWAN_PROD_00193 | Jan. 1 – June 30, 2024 | NovaBridge Solutions LLC | 5, 8, 12 | Q1 $3,373; Q2 $9,289; YTD $12,662; self-approved per Operating Agreement §5.3 |
| PRIV-001–004 | Withheld: 4 emails/memos between D. Kwan and R. Fossett (Aug. 5 – Sept. 8, 2024) | — | Aug. 5 – Sept. 8, 2024 | Withheld (privilege) | 13, 14, 15, 18 | Formal privilege log promised within 10 business days |

**Production scope notes from the cover letter.** (i) Request Nos. 16–22 (NovaBridge corporate records, the Operating Agreement, and entity-level financial records) were **not** produced; Respondent states he is "continuing to coordinate" with NovaBridge and co-member Jared Ostrowski and will supplement. (ii) Respondent produced only his **individual** accounts and objects to producing accounts "held solely by Petitioner." (iii) A formal privilege log is "forthcoming" within ten (10) business days. (iv) Respondent's own First Request for Production served on Petitioner (Sept. 18, 2024) remains outstanding.

---

## III. Summary of Figures

---

## IV. Flagged Inconsistencies, Omissions, and Red Flags

The following items are enumerated individually. Each is supported by the cited produced document. Items are grouped by category for ease of reference but each numbered item is a distinct issue.

### A. Income

**1. Material understatement of total income.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶16) reports total 2023 income of $359,000. The 2023 joint tax return (PROD-002) reflects Respondent's share of income at approximately $571,740 (W-2 $185,000 + K-1 ordinary business income $367,200 + net rental $2,940 + interest $1,840 + ordinary dividends $6,310 + capital gains $8,450). The Declaration understates Respondent's 2023 income by approximately **$212,740**. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶¶15–16; PROD-002 Form 1040 Lines 1a, 2b, 3b, 7, 8; Schedule K-1 Box 1; Schedule E Part I.*

**2. Conflation of K-1 distributions with K-1 pass-through income.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶15) describes "$174,000" as "K-1 Income / Distributions" and states it is "reflected on the Schedule K-1." The produced K-1 (PROD-002) reports **ordinary business income of $367,200 in Box 1** (the taxable pass-through income) and **cash distributions of $174,000 in Box 19a** (a return of capital, not income). The Declaration reports the distribution figure as income, understating taxable K-1 income by **$193,200**. For child-support and alimony purposes, the $367,200 pass-through income (not the $174,000 distribution) is the relevant figure. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶15, ¶16; PROD-002 Schedule K-1 Boxes 1 and 19a.*

**3. False statement that Respondent has "no other sources of income."** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶19) expressly states Respondent has no interest, dividend, rental, royalty, trust, or other investment income ("None"). The tax return (PROD-002) reports taxable interest ($1,840), ordinary dividends ($6,310), qualified dividends ($4,620), net capital gain ($8,450), and net rental real estate income ($2,940). The Crestline statements (PROD-003) show ongoing reinvested dividends throughout 2024, and the FCB statements (PROD-004) show ongoing $2,100/month tenant rent deposits. The "None" representation is directly contradicted by Respondent's own produced records. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶19; PROD-002 Form 1040 Lines 2b, 3a, 3b, 7, 8; PROD-003 (reinvested dividends); PROD-004 (tenant rent deposits).*

**4. Omission of rental income from 1035 Elm Street, Unit C.** The tax return (PROD-002, Schedule E Part I) reports $25,200 in rents received ($2,100/month × 12) and $2,940 net rental income from 1035 Elm Street, Unit C, "titled in the name of Darren Kwan." The FCB checking statements (PROD-004) confirm $2,100/month "ACH Deposit — Tenant Rent Payment" in April, May, June, and July 2024. The Declaration omits this income stream entirely. *Sources: PROD-002 Schedule E Part I; PROD-004 (Apr.–Jul. 2024 rent deposits); cf. PROD-001 ¶19.*

**5. Omission of the $9,600/year employer vehicle allowance.** Respondent's 2023 W-2 (PROD-002) reports in Box 14 a "Vehicle allowance" of $9,600 ($800/month employer-provided vehicle stipend paid by NovaBridge). This compensation/benefit is not disclosed anywhere in the Declaration's income section (which claims total income of $359,000 and "no other sources of income"). The allowance either constitutes additional income or offsets the auto expenses Respondent claims on his monthly expense schedule. *Sources: PROD-002 W-2 #1 Box 14; PROD-001 ¶¶16, 19.*

**6. 2024 payroll direct deposits do not reconcile to the declared $185,000 salary.** The FCB checking statements (PROD-004) show semi-monthly direct deposits from NovaBridge payroll of $7,115.38 (24 deposits/year = $170,769.12). Even treated as net pay, $7,115.38 is far too high to be the net of a $185,000 gross after the $22,500 401(k) deferral and the $38,220 federal withholding shown on the 2023 W-2 (which would yield net of roughly $4,300–$4,400 per check). The deposit level is instead consistent with a gross salary materially higher than $185,000, or with compensation not reflected on the W-2. The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶14) represents that 2024 salary "remains at the same level" as 2023 ($185,000). *Sources: PROD-004 (Mar.–Aug. 2024 payroll deposits); PROD-001 ¶14; PROD-002 W-2 #1.*

### B. Assets — Omission of 1035 Elm Street, Unit C

**7. Complete omission of a separately titled investment property.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶23) states: "Respondent does not hold any ownership interest, whether sole or joint, in any other real property" besides the marital residence. The produced records establish that Respondent owns 1035 Elm Street, Unit C, Fairview, Columbia 20147: (i) the 2023 tax return (PROD-002, Schedule E Part I) lists it as a rental property "titled in the name of Darren Kwan," 365 rental days, 0 personal use; (ii) the Meridian mortgage statements (PROD-005) identify Darren Kwan as sole borrower on Loan 7842-0163-ELM for that property; and (iii) the FCB checking statements (PROD-004) show both the $1,680/month Elm Street mortgage payment and the $2,100/month tenant rent. The property was acquired during the marriage (mortgage originated October 8, 2019) and is likely subject to equitable distribution. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶23; PROD-002 Schedule E Part I; PROD-005 (all three statements); PROD-004 (Meridian $1,680 ACH and tenant rent deposits).*

**8. Omission of the Elm Street mortgage from the liability schedule.** The Meridian mortgage statements (PROD-005) show an outstanding principal balance of approximately **$241,281.19** as of August 1, 2024 (original loan $279,000, 4.875% fixed, maturity Nov. 1, 2049), with $1,680/month PITI. The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶¶43–48) lists only the Birchwood mortgage ($218,400), the BMW auto loan ($22,100), and the credit card (~$8,500), totaling ~$249,000, and states Respondent "does not have any other outstanding debts." Including the Elm Street mortgage, Respondent's true liabilities approximate **$490,000** — roughly double the disclosed amount. *Sources: PROD-005 (Aug. 1, 2024 statement); PROD-001 ¶¶43, 48.*

**9. The one mortgage statement produced is for the undisclosed property, not the disclosed one.** The cover letter describes PROD-005 as mortgage statements for "1035 Elm Street, Unit C" — the property omitted from the Declaration. No mortgage statement was produced for the marital residence at 418 Birchwood Lane, which is the Declaration's single largest disclosed liability ($218,400). The Birchwood mortgage balance therefore cannot be verified from the production. *Sources: Cover letter (PROD-005 description); PROD-005; PROD-001 ¶¶20–22, 44.*

### C. Dissipation / Undisclosed Transfers (Post-Separation)

**10. $85,000 outgoing wire transfer to an undisclosed account.** The Crestline statements (PROD-003, June 2024) show an outgoing wire transfer of **$85,000.00 on June 15, 2024** to "Harborstone Financial Group," recipient account ending 2291 (Reference WT-2024-061524-0087), initiated by the account holder with verbal authorization. This transfer occurred two weeks after the Declaration's stated separation date of June 1, 2024. No account at Harborstone Financial Group is disclosed anywhere in the Declaration (PROD-001, ¶¶24–33 list only FCB, Crestline, and Pinnacle accounts). The Harborstone account is an undisclosed repository of marital funds. *Sources: PROD-003 June 2024 statement and YTD summary; PROD-001 ¶¶24–33.*

**11. Recurring transfers to an unidentified third party ("Kiera M.").** The FCB checking statements (PROD-004) show recurring Venmo payments to "Kiera M." totaling **$14,600** over the produced period: $2,800 (3/15), $3,100 (4/22), $2,500 (5/10), $3,200 (6/18), and $3,000 (7/14). These are sizable, regular transfers to an unidentified individual, continuing before and after separation, and are not disclosed or explained in the Declaration. The identity of "Kiera M." and the purpose of the transfers must be established. *Sources: PROD-004 (Mar.–July 2024 Venmo entries).*

**12. Large post-separation lump-sum 401(k) contribution.** The FCB checking statements (PROD-004) show regular 401(k) ACH transfers to Pinnacle Retirement Services of $1,437.50/month (March–July), followed by an anomalous **$14,644.60** transfer on August 1, 2024 ("Early Month") — roughly ten times the normal monthly amount and occurring post-separation. No 401(k) account statement was produced to verify the declared $412,800 balance (PROD-001, ¶30) or to show the effect of this contribution. *Sources: PROD-004 (Mar.–Aug. 2024 Pinnacle 401(k) ACH entries); PROD-001 ¶30.*

### D. Valuation-Date Misrepresentation

**13. Account balances reported as of August 1, 2024, not the June 1, 2024 separation date.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶56) represents that "[a]ll asset values and account balances reflected herein are approximate as of the date of separation, which Respondent identifies as on or about June 1, 2024." The produced statements show the reported balances are actually as of **August 1, 2024**: individual checking $8,215 (FCB 8/1/2024), individual savings $42,680 (FCB 8/1/2024), and Crestline brokerage $387,450 (Crestline 8/1/2024 valuation). *Sources: PROD-001 ¶56; PROD-004 combined summary (8/1/2024); PROD-003 Aug. 1, 2024 valuation.*

**14. The reported brokerage balance masks the $85,000 wire.** The Crestline account held **$468,695** as of June 1, 2024 (the stated separation date) but only **$387,450** as of August 1, 2024 — a decline of $81,245 driven principally by the $85,000 wire on June 15, 2024 (see Item 10). By reporting the post-wire August 1 balance as the "separation date" value, the Declaration understates this marital asset by approximately **$81,245**. *Sources: PROD-003 June 2024 statement (opening $468,695) and Aug. 1, 2024 valuation ($387,450); PROD-001 ¶29.*

**15. The reported checking balance masks post-separation outflows.** The individual checking account held **$26,869.56** on June 1, 2024 but only **$8,215.00** on August 1, 2024, after the $14,644.60 401(k) transfer, rent, and mortgage payments posted on August 1. The Declaration reports the lower August 1 figure. *Sources: PROD-004 June 2024 statement (opening $26,869.56) and Aug. 1, 2024 partial statement ($8,215.00); PROD-001 ¶27.*

### E. Credit Card

**16. Credit-card issuer misidentified in the Declaration.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶46) lists the credit-card creditor as "First National Card Services." The produced statements (PROD-006) are from **Fairview Community Bank** (Platinum Visa, account ending 8316). The creditor identified in the sworn Declaration does not match the produced account. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶46; PROD-006 (all statements).*

**17. Credit-card payment pattern materially misstated.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶46) states Respondent "typically pays between $500 and $1,000 per month" on a revolving balance of ~$8,500. The produced statements (PROD-006) show the card is **paid in full each month**: payments of $3,240.18 (received 5/1), $7,038.45 (6/1), $8,124.30 (7/1), and $12,486.75 (8/1). Actual payments are 3–12 times the declared range. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶46; PROD-006 (May–Aug. 2024 statements).*

**18. Credit-card payments do not reconcile to the produced checking account.** The card statements (PROD-006) reflect payments received on the 1st of each month ($3,240.18, $7,038.45, $8,124.30, $12,486.75; total $30,889.68). The FCB individual checking (PROD-004) shows separate "ACH — Fairview Community Bank Visa" debits on the 15th of $1,450 (3/22), $2,230 (4/15), $3,875 (5/15), $4,120 (6/15), and $5,480 (7/15) (total $17,155). The amounts and timing do not match, indicating the Platinum Visa (acct. 8316) is funded from a different, undisclosed account (possibly the joint account 4821 or the Harborstone account). *Sources: PROD-006 (payment-received lines); PROD-004 (FCB Visa ACH debits).*

**19. Possible second, undisclosed credit card.** Because the "Fairview Community Bank Visa" payments debited from the individual checking (Item 18) do not correspond to the produced Platinum Visa account 8316, the production may reflect a second FCB Visa card that is neither produced nor disclosed in the Declaration. All credit-card accounts in Respondent's name should be produced. *Sources: PROD-004 (FCB Visa ACH debits); PROD-006 (Platinum Visa 8316); PROD-001 ¶46.*

**20. Most recent card balance differs from the declared balance.** The August 2024 statement (PROD-006) shows a new balance of **$6,418.50** (due Sept. 25, 2024), whereas the Declaration (PROD-001, ¶46) reports an approximate revolving balance of **$8,500**. The declared figure is not supported by the most recent produced statement. *Sources: PROD-006 Aug. 2024 statement; PROD-001 ¶46.*

**21. Credit-card rewards points not disclosed as an asset.** The card statements (PROD-006) show a Platinum Rewards points balance growing from 42,316 (May) to **69,346** (August), accruing 7,000–12,000 points/month. These points have monetary/redemption value and are not listed among Respondent's assets in the Declaration. *Sources: PROD-006 (May–Aug. 2024 statements); PROD-001 ¶¶24–42.*

### F. Monthly Expenses

**22. Clothing expense grossly understated.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶49) budgets $200/month for "Clothing and apparel." The credit-card statements (PROD-006) show charges at Monarch Fine Clothiers of $2,340 (5/22), $1,650 (6/5), $1,890 (7/5), and $1,420 (8/9) — **$7,300 in four months (~$1,825/month)**, roughly nine times the declared amount. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶49; PROD-006 (May–Aug. 2024 Monarch charges).*

**23. Dining/entertainment and personal-care expenses understated; country-club dues undisclosed.** The Declaration budgets $600/month for "Dining out and entertainment" and $100/month for "Personal care and grooming" (PROD-001, ¶49). The card statements (PROD-006) show recurring charges at The Capital Grille, Ridgeline Steakhouse, and Bella Notte Ristorante (hundreds of dollars each, multiple times per month), Aura Salon & Spa ($295–$485/month), and **Fairview Country Club monthly dues of $425** (May–Aug., $1,700 total) — a recurring obligation absent from the Declaration's expense schedule. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶49; PROD-006 (May–Aug. 2024 restaurant, salon, and country-club charges).*

**24. Auto-insurance expense understated.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶49) budgets $210/month for auto insurance. The FCB checking statements (PROD-004) show Progressive auto-insurance ACH debits of **$312/month** (March–July), a $102/month discrepancy. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶49; PROD-004 (Mar.–July 2024 Progressive ACH).*

**25. The declared $3,500/month voluntary household contribution is not traceable in the produced records.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶51) states Respondent has voluntarily contributed $3,500/month since approximately June 2024 "directly to Petitioner or to the mortgage servicer." The produced individual checking (PROD-004) shows the $1,842 Birchwood mortgage payment to Meridian but no corresponding ~$1,658/month transfer to Petitioner that would complete the $3,500, and no $3,500 transfer to Petitioner. The funding source and recipients of the voluntary payments are not documented in the produced individual-account records. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶51; PROD-004 (Mar.–Aug. 2024).*

**26. Auto-loan payment not shown in the produced checking account.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶45) states the BMW auto loan ($22,100 balance) is serviced at $425/month through "Fairview Community Bank Auto Finance." No $425 auto-loan debit appears in the individual checking statements (PROD-004) for March–August 2024. The account from which the auto loan is paid is not produced. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶45; PROD-004 (no auto-loan debit).*

### G. Business / Governance

**27. Self-approval of expense reimbursements without co-member oversight.** The NovaBridge expense reports (PROD-007, Section 1) state that under Section 5.3 of the Operating Agreement, a Managing Member holding a majority interest (60% or greater) approves his own expense reimbursements "without requirement of separate board or member approval," and that Jared Ostrowski's co-member review is "N/A — not required." Respondent thus controls his own reimbursement authority, creating a vehicle to convert company funds to personal use disguised as business expenses (and a potential source of undocumented income/distributions). *Sources: PROD-007 §1, §5.*

**28. The Operating Agreement (referenced in PROD-007) was not produced.** Section 5.3 of the NovaBridge Operating Agreement is cited and relied upon in the expense reports but the Operating Agreement itself was not produced; the cover letter states Request Nos. 16–22 (which include the Operating Agreement and entity-level records) remain outstanding. The agreement's transfer restrictions, buyout provisions, and compensation/distribution provisions are directly relevant to valuation and dissipation. *Sources: PROD-007 §1; Cover letter (RFP Nos. 16–22).*

**29. Expense reimbursements do not reconcile to bank deposits.** The expense reports (PROD-007) show Q1 $3,373 (approved 4/8) and Q2 $9,289 (approved 7/3), totaling $12,662. The FCB checking (PROD-004) shows only two "NovaBridge Solutions LLC Expense Reimb." deposits: $3,400 (4/10) and $2,150 (5/20), totaling $5,550 — leaving approximately **$7,112** of reported reimbursements unaccounted for in the produced bank records. Additionally, the 4/10 deposit ($3,400) does not match the Q1 report total ($3,373), a $27 discrepancy. *Sources: PROD-007 §4; PROD-004 (4/10 and 5/20 reimbursement deposits).*

**30. Same-day, near-identical "personal devices" purchases on the personal card and as a company reimbursement.** On **May 17, 2024**, the credit card (PROD-006) shows a $2,149.50 charge at "Pinnacle Electronics — Personal Devices," and the Q2 expense report (PROD-007) shows a $2,150.00 charge on the same date at "TechVault Solutions" for "Technology Equipment — Personal Devices" (tablet, smartphone, accessories). The same date, near-identical amount, and identical "personal devices" description suggest possible double payment or commingling of personal and business purchases; the underlying receipts should be examined. *Sources: PROD-006 May 2024 (5/17 Pinnacle Electronics); PROD-007 §3 (5/17 TechVault).*

**31. Business interest not valued.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶37) states the value of Respondent's 60% NovaBridge interest "has not been formally appraised" and reserves the right to engage a valuator. The K-1 (PROD-002) reports an ending tax-basis capital account of $675,800 and recourse liabilities of $36,400, and the entity summary shows $322,000 retained at the entity level (60% share $193,200). No valuation has been provided. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶37; PROD-002 Schedule K-1 (capital account, liabilities) and entity summary.*

### H. Production / Internal Inconsistencies

**32. Conflicting separation dates.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶5) states the parties "effectively separated on or about June 1, 2024." The cover letter's privilege log describes withheld communications "following the parties' separation on August 1, 2024" (PRIV-001) and "post-separation August 1, 2024" (PRIV-002). The date of separation is a controlling date for identifying the marital estate and for characterizing the $85,000 wire (Item 10) and other transfers; the discrepancy must be resolved. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶5; Cover letter (PRIV-001, PRIV-002).*

**33. Conflicting filing date for the Sworn Financial Declaration.** The cover letter states the Declaration was "filed with the Court on or about October 4, 2024," while the Declaration itself states "Filed: September 20, 2024" and is notarized and certified as served on September 20, 2024. *Sources: Cover letter (PROD-001); PROD-001 (Filed line, notarization, certificate of service).*

**34. Case-number and court-name error on the FCB production stamp.** The FCB statements (PROD-004) bear a production stamp reading "Case No. 2024-DR-**00847**, Circuit Court of **Columbia, Fairview County**." The correct caption (cover letter; PROD-001; PROD-007) is Case No. 2024-DR-**01847**, Circuit Court of **Fairview County, Columbia**. The bank production misstates both the case number and the court name. *Sources: PROD-004 (production stamp); Cover letter; PROD-001; PROD-007.*

**35. Inconsistent Bates-numbering schemes.** The tax-return excerpts use the prefix "DK-PROD-" (DK-PROD-0045 – 0058), while the Crestline, FCB, and NovaBridge productions use "KWAN_PROD_" (e.g., KWAN_PROD_00142, 0401, 00187). The mixed prefixes complicate indexing and citation. *Sources: PROD-002 (DK-PROD-); PROD-003, PROD-004, PROD-007 (KWAN_PROD_).*

**36. Missing Bates ranges on two productions.** The credit-card statements (PROD-006) and the Elm Street mortgage statements (PROD-005) do not bear or state any Bates-stamp range, unlike the other five sets. *Sources: PROD-005; PROD-006 (no Bates range identified).*

**37. Undated cover letter.** The transmittal email's "Date:" field is blank; the production cover letter is undated. *Source: Cover letter (Date field).*

**38. Conflicting contact information for Respondent's counsel.** The Declaration (PROD-001, prepared-by block) lists Raymond Fossett at telephone (571) 555-0184, fax (571) 555-0185, email rfossett@**hfylaw**.com. The cover letter signature block lists the same attorney at telephone (571) 555-4200, fax (571) 555-4201, email rfossett@**hfy-law**.com. The telephone, fax, and email domain all differ. *Sources: PROD-001 (prepared-by block); Cover letter (signature block).*

**39. Brokerage account tax ID does not match the account holder's SSN.** The Crestline statements (PROD-003) list the account Tax ID as ●●●-●●-**8341**. Respondent's SSN, per the tax return (PROD-002), ends in **4718**; the suffix 8341 instead matches the dependent child Ethan Kwan's SSN (XXX-XX-8341). The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶29) represents the account is "held in Respondent's name alone." The mismatch should be reconciled (possible titling/custodial issue or statement error). *Sources: PROD-003 (account information, all statements); PROD-002 (SSNs for Darren and Ethan Kwan); PROD-001 ¶29.*

**40. Brokerage and bank mailing addresses remained the marital residence through August 2024.** Although the Declaration (PROD-001, ¶2) states Respondent moved to 2200 Harbor Point Drive on or about June 1, 2024, the Crestline statements (PROD-003) and FCB statements (PROD-004) continue to show the mailing address as 418 Birchwood Lane through August 1, 2024 (only the credit card, PROD-006, shows an address change to Harbor Point effective the July cycle). The continued use of the marital-residence address for investment and bank mail should be confirmed as merely administrative. *Sources: PROD-003 (all statements); PROD-004 (all statements); PROD-006 (July–Aug. address change); PROD-001 ¶2.*

### I. Tax-Return Technical Items

**41. K-1 interest income may not be reflected on Form 1040.** The Schedule K-1 (PROD-002) reports $1,240 of interest income in Box 5 (pass-through from NovaBridge). Form 1040 Line 2b reports total taxable interest of $1,840, which Schedule B attributes entirely to "accounts at Fairview Community Bank." The K-1 Box 5 interest does not appear to be included in Line 2b, suggesting possible underreported interest income of $1,240 (or an incomplete Schedule B summary). *Sources: PROD-002 Schedule K-1 Box 5; Form 1040 Line 2b; Schedule B summary.*

**42. QBI deduction appears to be zero despite Form 8995 being filed.** The tax computation (PROD-002) shows taxable income (Line 15, $564,240) equal to AGI ($612,960) less itemized deductions ($48,720) only — i.e., no Qualified Business Income deduction appears on Line 13, even though Form 8995 was filed and NovaBridge generated $367,200 of QBI with substantial W-2 wages. A nonzero QBI deduction might be expected at these facts; Form 8995 was not produced. *Sources: PROD-002 Form 1040 Lines 11–15; Schedule K-1 Box 1; "Additional Schedules" summary (Form 8995 referenced but not excerpted).*

**43. Only excerpts of the 47-page return were produced.** The tax-return cover sheet (PROD-002) states the complete return comprises 47 pages and that Schedule A, Schedule B (detail), Schedule D, Schedule SE, and Form 8995 were filed but are "not produced in these excerpts" (only summarized). The full return and all schedules should be obtained. *Sources: PROD-002 cover sheet and "Additional Schedules" summary.*

### J. Production Completeness Gaps

**44. Joint account statements (FCB 4821 and 4822) not produced.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶¶25–26) discloses a joint checking account (ending 4821, $12,340) and joint savings account (ending 4822, $28,750) held jointly with Petitioner. No statements for either joint account were produced. As a joint account holder, Respondent has possession, custody, or control of these records; the cover letter's objection to producing "accounts held solely by Petitioner" does not extend to joint accounts. *Sources: PROD-001 ¶¶25–26, 32; Cover letter (general reservations); PROD-004 (individual accounts only).*

**45. 401(k) account statement (Pinnacle Retirement, acct. 3310) not produced.** The Declaration (PROD-001, ¶30) reports a 401(k) balance of $412,800. No 401(k) statement was produced, and the FCB records (PROD-004) show ongoing contributions including the anomalous $14,644.60 transfer on 8/1/2024 (Item 12). *Sources: PROD-001 ¶30; PROD-004 (Pinnacle 401(k) ACH entries).*

**46. Marital-residence mortgage statement not produced.** No statement for the Birchwood Lane mortgage (the Declaration's largest disclosed liability, $218,400) was produced; only the Elm Street mortgage was produced (Item 9). *Sources: PROD-001 ¶¶20–22, 44; Cover letter (PROD-005 = Elm Street only).*

**47. Auto-loan documentation not produced.** No statement or loan document for the BMW auto loan ($22,100, FCB Auto Finance) was produced, and the $425/month payment is not visible in the produced checking account (Item 26). *Sources: PROD-001 ¶45; PROD-004.*

**48. Entity-level NovaBridge records not produced (RFP Nos. 16–22).** The cover letter confirms the NovaBridge Operating Agreement, entity-level financial records, Form 1065, and corporate records were not produced and will be "supplemented." These are essential to value the 60% interest, to test the $174,000 distribution against entity cash flow, and to assess whether additional distributions or disguised compensation occurred. *Sources: Cover letter (RFP Nos. 16–22); PROD-002 entity summary; PROD-007 §1.*

**49. Formal privilege log not yet produced.** The cover letter withholds PRIV-001 through PRIV-004 on attorney-client/work-product grounds but states a formal privilege log "in full compliance with the applicable rules is forthcoming … within ten (10) business days." The log had not been served as of this production. *Source: Cover letter (Privilege Log section).*

**50. Petitioner's reciprocal production outstanding.** The cover letter notes that Respondent's First Request for Production served on Petitioner (Sept. 18, 2024) "remains outstanding." Petitioner's production (including her 403(b) statements, her FCB joint-account access, and her employment records) should be pursued. *Source: Cover letter (final paragraph).*

---

## V. Recommended Follow-Up Discovery

The following discovery steps are recommended, keyed to the issues above.

### A. Document demands / meet-and-confer on the existing production

1. **Demand supplementation and correction of the Sworn Financial Declaration (PROD-001)** to (i) report K-1 ordinary business income ($367,200) rather than distributions ($174,000); (ii) add the omitted interest, dividend, capital-gain, and rental income; (iii) add the 1035 Elm Street property and its mortgage and rental income; (iv) add the $9,600 vehicle allowance; (v) correct the credit-card creditor to Fairview Community Bank and the payment pattern; (vi) restate all account balances as of the June 1, 2024 separation date (or such other date as the Court fixes); and (vii) disclose the Harborstone Financial Group account (ending 2291). Consider a motion to compel and for sanctions if not corrected.

2. **Meet and confer on the separation date** (Item 32). The June 1, 2024 vs. August 1, 2024 conflict must be resolved because it controls characterization of the $85,000 wire and other transfers. Request a sworn statement of the factual basis for each date.

3. **Demand production of the joint accounts** (FCB 4821 and 4822) (Item 44). Respondent is a joint account holder and the "solely Petitioner's accounts" objection does not apply.

4. **Demand the full 47-page 2023 tax return** with all schedules (A, B detail, D, SE, Form 8995) and the corresponding 2022 return (Items 41–43). Also demand 2024 quarterly estimated-tax payment records (the $10,550 Q1 estimate paid from Crestline on 4/15/2024).

5. **Demand the formal privilege log** (Item 49) if not served within the represented ten business days; scrutinize PRIV-003 ("Preliminary analysis of marital estate," Aug. 20, 2024) for any portion not protected (e.g., factual financial data vs. legal advice).

### B. Targeted document requests / subpoenas to third parties

6. **Subpoena Harborstone Financial Group** for all records of account ending 2291 (and any other accounts in Respondent's name or controlled by him) from January 2023 to present (Items 10, 18). This is the single most important follow-up: it will show where the $85,000 went and may reveal the funding source for the credit card and other payments.

7. **Subpoena Fairview Community Bank** for (i) all accounts in Darren Kwan's name (including any second Visa card and the auto-loan account) and all joint accounts (4821, 4822) from January 2023 to present; and (ii) the auto-loan (FCB Auto Finance) note and payment history (Items 18, 19, 26, 44, 47).

8. **Subpoena Pinnacle Retirement Services** for the 401(k) account (ending 3310) statements and transaction history from January 2023 to present, including loan/withdrawal records and the August 1, 2024 $14,644.60 transaction (Items 12, 45).

9. **Subpoena Meridian Home Lending** for the **418 Birchwood Lane** mortgage note, payment history, and current payoff (Item 46), in addition to the Elm Street loan already produced.

10. **Subpoena Crestline Wealth Advisors** for the complete account history (account 9956) from account inception, all wire/transfer records, the advisory agreement, and confirmation of the account's tax ID/registration (Items 10, 39, 40).

11. **Subpoena NovaBridge Solutions LLC** (and serve on Jared Ostrowski) for the Operating Agreement (with all amendments), Form 1065 and full partnership return for 2022 and 2023, entity-level financial statements, bank statements, the general ledger, member capital accounts, distribution registers, and all expense-reimbursement records for both members (Items 27, 28, 29, 31, 48).

12. **Serve a discovery request / deposition notice on Respondent** requiring identification of "Kiera M." and the purpose of the $14,600 in Venmo transfers, and production of all Venmo/Zelle/CashApp and peer-transfer records (Item 11).

### C. Depositions and expert work

13. **Depose Darren Kwan** on the inconsistencies in Items 1–15, 22–30, and 32–33, with the produced documents marked as exhibits; focus on the Elm Street omission, the $85,000 wire, the "Kiera M." transfers, the self-approved expense reimbursements, the separation date, and the income/distribution distinction.

14. **Depose Jared Ostrowski** (co-member, 40%) on NovaBridge distributions, compensation, the Operating Agreement, expense-reimbursement practices, and any knowledge of Respondent's personal financial conduct (Items 27–31, 48).

15. **Retain a forensic accountant** to (i) reconstruct Respondent's actual 2023 and 2024 income from the bank, brokerage, and tax records; (ii) trace the $85,000 wire and the "Kiera M." transfers; (iii) reconcile the credit-card and expense-reimbursement flows; and (iv) value the 60% NovaBridge interest (Items 1, 2, 10, 11, 18, 29, 31).

16. **Obtain a formal appraisal** of the marital residence (418 Birchwood Lane) and the Elm Street condominium (1035 Elm Street, Unit C), and a business valuation of NovaBridge (Items 7, 9, 31).

### D. Motion practice

17. **Consider a motion for a preliminary injunction / freezing order** or an accounting under the applicable Columbia family-code provisions to prevent further dissipation, given the $85,000 post-separation wire, the escalating credit-card spending, and the self-approved reimbursement authority (Items 10, 17, 22–23, 27).

18. **Consider a motion to compel** the RFP Nos. 16–22 productions (Operating Agreement and entity records) on an expedited schedule, and to compel the joint-account and 401(k) statements (Items 44–48).

19. **Preserve a record for sanctions / adverse inference** should Respondent fail to correct the Declaration's material misstatements and omissions identified in Items 1–9, 13–15.

---

## VI. Conclusion

The production, while facially responsive to seven categories of requests, is internally contradictory and materially incomplete. Respondent's own tax return (PROD-002) and bank/brokerage records (PROD-003, PROD-004, PROD-005) directly refute core representations in his Sworn Financial Declaration (PROD-001): the Declaration omits a separately titled rental property and its mortgage and income; understates 2023 income by roughly $213,000 by reporting K-1 distributions as income and ignoring investment and rental income; reports post-separation (August 1, 2024) account balances as separation-date values, masking an $85,000 wire to an undisclosed Harborstone Financial Group account; and understates monthly expenses and liabilities. The records further reveal recurring transfers to an unidentified "Kiera M." ($14,600), a self-approved business-expense reimbursement structure, and a non-reconciling credit-card payment stream that implies an undisclosed funding account. Immediate follow-up discovery — particularly a subpoena to Harborstone Financial Group, production of the joint and 401(k) accounts, the NovaBridge Operating Agreement and entity records, and a deposition of Respondent — is warranted, together with a demand that the Sworn Financial Declaration be corrected under oath.

*Prepared for internal use. This memorandum is attorney work product and contains privileged and confidential information.*"}

Birch LLP DATE: [Date of review] RE: Torres-Kwan v. Kwan, Case
No. 2024-DR-01847 (Cir. Ct. Fairview County, Columbia; Hon. Patricia
Yoon-Callahan) — Review of Respondent Darren Kwan’s Discovery Production
(PROD-001 through PROD-007) and Recommended Follow-Up Discovery

I. Introduction and Scope

This memorandum catalogs, summarizes, and analyzes the document
production served by Respondent Darren Kwan (“Respondent”) through
counsel Raymond Fossett (Hargrove Fossett & Yates LLP) in response to
Petitioner Melinda Torres-Kwan’s (“Petitioner”) First Request for
Production of Documents, Nos. 1–22. The production consists of a
transmittal email (cover letter), seven (7) document sets designated
PROD-001 through PROD-007, and a summary privilege log identifying four
(4) withheld items (PRIV-001 through PRIV-004).

The production is reviewed below in four parts: (A) an exhibit catalog;
(B) a summary of the financial figures reported; (C) a detailed,
itemized list of inconsistencies, omissions, and red flags identified by
cross-referencing the produced documents against each other; and (D)
recommended follow-up discovery. The central finding is that
Respondent’s Sworn Financial Declaration (PROD-001) materially
understates income, omits a separately titled investment real-property
asset and its associated mortgage and rental income, and reports account
balances as of August 1, 2024 while representing them as separation-date
(June 1, 2024) values — thereby masking an $85,000 post-separation wire
transfer to an undisclosed account at Harborstone Financial Group. The
produced records also reveal a pattern of large, recurring transfers to
an unidentified third party (“Kiera M.”), escalating personal spending
inconsistent with the declared expense schedule, and a business-expense
reimbursement structure that permits Respondent to self-approve his own
reimbursements without co-member oversight.

IV. Flagged Inconsistencies, Omissions, and Red Flags

The following items are enumerated individually. Each is supported by
the cited produced document. Items are grouped by category for ease of
reference but each numbered item is a distinct issue.

A. Income

1. Material understatement of total income. The Declaration (PROD-001,
¶16) reports total 2023 income of $359,000. The 2023 joint tax return
(PROD-002) reflects Respondent’s share of income at approximately
$571,740 (W-2 $185,000 + K-1 ordinary business income $367,200 + net
rental $2,940 + interest $1,840 + ordinary dividends $6,310 + capital
gains $8,450). The Declaration understates Respondent’s 2023 income by
approximately $212,740. Sources: PROD-001 ¶¶15–16; PROD-002 Form 1040
Lines 1a, 2b, 3b, 7, 8; Schedule K-1 Box 1; Schedule E Part I.

2. Conflation of K-1 distributions with K-1 pass-through income. The
Declaration (PROD-001, ¶15) describes “$174,000” as “K-1 Income /
Distributions” and states it is “reflected on the Schedule K-1.” The
produced K-1 (PROD-002) reports ordinary business income of $367,200 in
Box 1 (the taxable pass-through income) and cash distributions of
$174,000 in Box 19a (a return of capital, not income). The Declaration
reports the distribution figure as income, understating taxable K-1
income by $193,200. For child-support and alimony purposes, the $367,200
pass-through income (not the $174,000 distribution) is the relevant
figure. Sources: PROD-001 ¶15, ¶16; PROD-002 Schedule K-1 Boxes 1 and
19a.

3. False statement that Respondent has “no other sources of income.” The
Declaration (PROD-001, ¶19) expressly states Respondent has no interest,
dividend, rental, royalty, trust, or other investment income (“None”).
The tax return (PROD-002) reports taxable interest ($1,840), ordinary
dividends ($6,310), qualified dividends ($4,620), net capital gain
($8,450), and net rental real estate income ($2,940). The Crestline
statements (PROD-003) show ongoing reinvested dividends throughout 2024,
and the FCB statements (PROD-004) show ongoing $2,100/month tenant rent
deposits. The “None” representation is directly contradicted by
Respondent’s own produced records. Sources: PROD-001 ¶19; PROD-002 Form
1040 Lines 2b, 3a, 3b, 7, 8; PROD-003 (reinvested dividends); PROD-004
(tenant rent deposits).

4. Omission of rental income from 1035 Elm Street, Unit C. The tax
return (PROD-002, Schedule E Part I) reports $25,200 in rents received
($2,100/month × 12) and $2,940 net rental income from 1035 Elm Street,
Unit C, “titled in the name of Darren Kwan.” The FCB checking statements
(PROD-004) confirm $2,100/month “ACH Deposit — Tenant Rent Payment” in
April, May, June, and July 2024. The Declaration omits this income
stream entirely. Sources: PROD-002 Schedule E Part I; PROD-004
(Apr.–Jul. 2024 rent deposits); cf. PROD-001 ¶19.

5. Omission of the $9,600/year employer vehicle allowance. Respondent’s
2023 W-2 (PROD-002) reports in Box 14 a “Vehicle allowance” of $9,600

14. Depose Jared Ostrowski (co-member, 40%) on NovaBridge distributions,
    compensation, the Operating Agreement, expense-reimbursement
    practices, and any knowledge of Respondent’s personal financial
    conduct (Items 27–31, 48).

15. Retain a forensic accountant to (i) reconstruct Respondent’s actual
    2023 and 2024 income from the bank, brokerage, and tax records; (ii)
    trace the $85,000 wire and the “Kiera M.” transfers; (iii) reconcile

16. Obtain a formal appraisal of the marital residence (418 Birchwood

17. Con

D. Motion practice

17. Consider a motion for a preliminary injunction / freezing order or
    an accounting under the applicable Columbia family-code provisions
    to prevent further dissipation, given the $85,000 post-separation
    wire, the escalating credit-card spending, and the self-approved
    reimbursement authority (Items 10, 17, 22–23, 27).

18. Consider a motion to compel the RFP Nos. 16–22 productions
    (Operating Agreement and entity records) on an expedited schedule,
    and to compel the joint-account and 401(k) statements (Items 44–48).

19. Preserve a record for sanctions / adverse inference should
    Respondent fail to correct the Declaration’s material misstatements
    and omissions identified in Items 1–9, 13–15.

VI. Conclusion

The production, while facially responsive to seven categories of
requests, is internally contradictory and materially incomplete.
Respondent’s own tax return (PROD-002) and bank/brokerage records
(PROD-003, PROD-004, PROD-005) directly refute core representations in
his Sworn Financial Declaration (PROD-001): the Declaration omits a
separately titled rental property and its mortgage and income;
understates 2023 income by roughly $213,000 by reporting K-1
distributions as income and ignoring investment and rental income;
reports post-separation (August 1, 2024) account balances as
separation-date values, masking an $85,000 wire to an undisclosed
Harborstone Financial Group account; and understates monthly expenses
and liabilities. The records further reveal recurring transfers to an
unidentified “Kiera M.” ($14,600), a self-approved business-expense
reimbursement structure, and a non-reconciling credit-card payment
stream that implies an undisclosed funding account. Immediate follow-up
discovery — particularly a subpoena to Harborstone Financial Group,
production of the joint and 401(k) accounts, the NovaBridge Operating
Agreement and entity records, and a deposition of Respondent — is
warranted, together with a demand that the Sworn Financial Declaration
be corrected under oath.

Prepared for internal use. This memorandum is attorney work product and
contains privileged and confidential information.

text:
The deliverable is complete and validated. Here is a summary of what I produced.

## Deliverable
**`output/exhibit-summary-memo.docx`** — an internal attorney-work-product memorandum reviewing Respondent Darren Kwan's discovery production in *Torres-Kwan v. Kwan*, Case No. 2024-DR-01847. The .docx is schema-valid (passed `validate.py`).

## What the memo contains

**I. Introduction & Scope** — frames the production (cover letter + PROD-001–007 + privilege log) and states the central finding up front.

**II. Exhibit Catalog** — a table cataloging every exhibit with its Bates range, date coverage, custodian/producer, responsive RFP numbers, and key contents (including the cover letter, all seven PROD sets, and PRIV-001–004).

**III. Summary of Figures** — six tables summarizing income (per the Declaration vs. per the tax return), assets, liabilities, monthly expenses, and NovaBridge business figures.

**IV. Flagged Inconsistencies, Omissions & Red Flags** — **50 individually enumerated items** grouped A–J, each citing the source document. Highlights:
- Income understated by ~$213,000 (K-1 *distributions* $174K reported instead of *pass-through income* $367K; rental/interest/dividend/capital-gain income omitted; "no other income" representation contradicted by the tax return)
- Complete omission of the separately titled **1035 Elm Street** rental property, its ~$241K mortgage, and its $2,100/mo rental income
- **$85,000 post-separation wire** (6/15/2024) to an undisclosed Harborstone Financial Group account
- **$14,600 in recurring Venmo transfers** to an unidentified "Kiera M."
- Valuation-date misrepresentation (August 1 balances reported as June 1 separation-date values, masking the wire)
- Credit-card creditor misidentified; payments don't reconcile to the produced checking account (implied undisclosed funding source/second card)
- Self-approved expense reimbursements under Operating Agreement §5.3; non-reconciling reimbursements; a same-day "personal devices" double-purchase
- Conflicting separation dates (June 1 vs. August 1) and filing dates; case-number/court-name error on the bank production; mixed Bates prefixes; brokerage tax-ID matching the minor child's SSN
- Production gaps: joint accounts, 401(k), Birchwood mortgage, auto loan, NovaBridge entity records/Operating Agreement, formal privilege log

**V. Recommended Follow-Up Discovery** — 19 specific steps across document demands/meet-and-confer, third-party subpoenas (Harborstone, FCB, Pinnacle, Meridian, Crestline, NovaBridge), depositions (Kwan, Ostrowski), forensic accountant and appraisals, and motion practice (injunction/freezing order, motion to compel, sanctions record).

**VI. Conclusion** — synthesizes the contradictions and prioritizes next steps.

Before you finish, run a mandatory COMPLETENESS AUDIT of your deliverable(s) against the source record. Do not assume your first draft covered everything — the most common failure on this kind of task is a deliverable that is polished but omits distinct, individually-graded points the record supports.

Do this now, as real tool calls (not in your head):
1. RE-LIST every distinct issue/requirement the source documents raise. Re-read the key source files and enumerate each separate problem, gap, discrepancy, nonconformance, or required element — one per item, not bundled. Include the ones you already wrote about AND any you have not yet addressed.
2. CROSS-CHECK your current deliverable against that list. For each item, is it actually present and explicitly addressed in the output file? Open the output file and verify by content, not memory. List every item that is missing, vague, bundled into another point, or only implied.
3. CHECK EACH REQUIRED OUTPUT / DIMENSION: confirm every deliverable the task asked for exists, and that any required comparison, matrix, or dimension (e.g. every jurisdiction, every party, every provision, every document in the file) is covered — do not drop a category because it seemed minor.
4. FILL THE GAPS: use edit/write to add every missing item with its own explicit treatment, drawing the specific facts from the source documents (cite the source). Do not fabricate issues the record does not support; do add the ones it does that you skipped.
5. Re-validate any binary deliverable per its skill manual, then finish.

If a thorough re-read finds nothing missing, say so briefly and finish. Otherwise, make the additions now.

**[RESPONDENT\'S SWORN FINANCIAL DECLARATION]{.underline}**

 

Filed: September 20, 2024

Prepared by: Raymond Fossett, Esq. Hargrove Fossett & Yates LLP 1600 Executive Tower, 8th Floor Fairview, Columbia 20142 Counsel for Respondent

 

Respondent, DARREN KWAN, by and through his undersigned counsel, Raymond Fossett of Hargrove Fossett & Yates LLP, hereby submits this Sworn Financial Declaration pursuant to Columbia Family Code § 9-207 and Fairview County Circuit Court Local Rule 12.04, and in compliance with the Court\'s Scheduling Order entered on September 10, 2024. Respondent states as follows:

**[I. PERSONAL INFORMATION]{.underline}**

1\. **Full Legal Name.** Respondent\'s full legal name is Darren Kwan. Respondent is forty-seven (47) years of age.

2\. **Current Residential Address.** Respondent currently resides at 2200 Harbor Point Drive, Unit 14B, Fairview, Columbia 20148. This is a leased condominium unit that Respondent began occupying following the parties\' separation. The monthly lease payment for this unit is Four Thousand Seven Hundred Fifty Dollars (\$4,750.00) per month, and the lease term is twelve (12) months commencing on or about June 1, 2024.

3\. **Former Marital Address.** The former marital residence of the parties is located at 418 Birchwood Lane, Fairview, Columbia 20145. The Petitioner and the parties\' minor children currently reside at this address.

4\. **Date of Marriage.** The parties were married on June 14, 2009, in Fairview County, State of Columbia.

5\. **Date of Separation.** The parties effectively separated on or about June 1, 2024, at which time the marital relationship became irretrievably broken. From approximately that date forward, the parties ceased to cohabit as husband and wife, maintained separate financial arrangements, and conducted their affairs independently of one another.

6\. **Date of Filing.** Petitioner, Melinda Torres-Kwan, initiated this dissolution proceeding by filing a Petition for Dissolution of Marriage on September 3, 2024.

7\. **Length of Marriage.** The marriage has been of approximately fifteen (15) years\' duration.

8\. **Minor Children.** The parties are the parents of two (2) minor children:

> \(a\) Ethan Kwan, date of birth March 2, 2011, currently thirteen (13) years of age; and
>
> \(b\) Sofia Kwan, date of birth November 18, 2014, currently nine (9) years of age.

Both minor children currently reside at the marital home located at 418 Birchwood Lane, Fairview, Columbia 20145, with the Petitioner. Respondent exercises regular parenting time with the children pursuant to the parties\' informal arrangement pending entry of a temporary custody order by this Court.

9\. **Respondent\'s Employer.** Respondent is employed by NovaBridge Solutions LLC, located at 900 Commerce Park Boulevard, Suite 310, Fairview, Columbia 20146. Respondent holds the title of Co-Founder and Chief Technology Officer.

10\. **Petitioner\'s Employment.** To the best of Respondent\'s knowledge and belief, Petitioner Melinda Torres-Kwan is employed as a high school art teacher at Fairview Regional High School, employed through the Fairview Regional School District.

11\. **Petitioner\'s Counsel.** Petitioner is represented in this matter by Sarah Whitfield, Esq., of Calloway & Birch LLP, 75 Courthouse Square, Suite 400, Fairview, Columbia 20140.

**[II. INCOME AND EMPLOYMENT]{.underline}**

**A. Employment Income**

12\. Respondent is employed as the Co-Founder and Chief Technology Officer of NovaBridge Solutions LLC (\"NovaBridge\"), a Delaware limited liability company formed on January 15, 2016. NovaBridge maintains its principal office at 900 Commerce Park Boulevard, Suite 310, Fairview, Columbia 20146. The company provides enterprise software solutions and technology consulting services to corporate clients.

13\. Respondent co-founded NovaBridge together with Jared Ostrowski, who serves as Chief Executive Officer. Respondent holds a sixty percent (60%) membership interest in NovaBridge, and Mr. Ostrowski holds the remaining forty percent (40%) membership interest. As a member of a limited liability company taxed as a pass-through entity, Respondent receives compensation in two forms: a W-2 salary for services rendered in his capacity as Chief Technology Officer, and periodic distributions from the company as a member.

14\. For the calendar year 2023, Respondent\'s W-2 salary from NovaBridge Solutions LLC was One Hundred Eighty-Five Thousand Dollars (\$185,000.00) per annum, paid on a bi-monthly basis. Respondent\'s current 2024 W-2 salary remains at the same level and has not been increased from the 2023 rate.

**B. Business Income / Distributions**

15\. In addition to his W-2 salary, Respondent received K-1 income from NovaBridge Solutions LLC during the 2023 calendar year. The K-1 distributions received by Respondent from NovaBridge during 2023 totaled One Hundred Seventy-Four Thousand Dollars (\$174,000.00). These distributions were received periodically throughout the year and are reflected on the Schedule K-1 issued to Respondent by NovaBridge in connection with the 2023 federal and state tax returns. The amount and timing of K-1 distributions vary from year to year and are determined by the members based on available cash flow, working capital requirements, and the operational needs of the business. There is no guaranteed or fixed distribution amount, and future distributions may be more or less than the amount received in 2023.

**C. Total Annual Income**

16\. Based on the foregoing, Respondent\'s total annual income for the calendar year 2023 was as follows:

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Source**                                              **Annual Amount**
  ------------------------------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  W-2 Salary (NovaBridge Solutions LLC)                   \$185,000.00

  K-1 Income / Distributions (NovaBridge Solutions LLC)   \$174,000.00

  **Total Annual Income**                                 **\$359,000.00**
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

17\. Respondent represents that the foregoing constitutes a full and accurate statement of his income for the 2023 calendar year. Respondent\'s 2024 income is anticipated to be at approximately the same level, although the actual K-1 distribution amount for 2024 will not be determinable until the close of the fiscal year.

**D. Petitioner\'s Income (As Known to Respondent)**

18\. To the best of Respondent\'s knowledge and belief, Petitioner Melinda Torres-Kwan earns an approximate gross annual salary of Sixty-Seven Thousand Two Hundred Dollars (\$67,200.00) as a high school art teacher employed by the Fairview Regional School District. Petitioner has been employed in this capacity for approximately eleven (11) years. Respondent believes Petitioner receives standard benefits through her employment, including health insurance coverage for herself and the parties\' minor children, and participation in a retirement savings plan administered through Pinnacle Retirement Services.

**E. Other Income**

19\. Other than the income described in Subsections A through C above, Respondent has no other sources of income, including but not limited to interest income, dividend income, rental income, royalty income, trust income, or income from any other business venture or investment. **None.**

**[III. ASSETS]{.underline}**

**A. Real Property**

20\. The following is a complete list of all real property in which Respondent holds an ownership interest, whether individually or jointly with another party:

**C. Business Interests**

34\. Respondent holds a sixty percent (60%) membership interest in NovaBridge Solutions LLC, a Delaware limited liability company formed on January 15, 2016. NovaBridge maintains its principal office at 900 Commerce Park Boulevard, Suite 310, Fairview, Columbia 20146. 35\. Respondent co-founded NovaBridge together with Jared Ostrowski, who holds the remaining forty percent (40%) membership interest and serves as the company\'s Chief Executive Officer. Respondent serves as the Chief Technology Officer and is responsible for product development, engineering oversight, and technology strategy. Both members are actively involved in the day-to-day management and operations of the business.

36\. For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, NovaBridge reported gross revenues of approximately Four Million Two Hundred Eighty Thousand Dollars (\$4,280,000.00). For the prior fiscal year ending December 31, 2022, NovaBridge reported gross revenues of approximately Three Million Nine Hundred Fifteen Thousand Dollars (\$3,915,000.00). The company has experienced steady growth since its founding, though future revenues remain subject to market conditions, client retention, and competitive pressures.

37\. The value of Respondent\'s membership interest in NovaBridge Solutions LLC has not been formally appraised. 40\. The 2020 Honda CR-V is titled in Petitioner\'s name and is currently in Petitioner\'s possession. **E. Other Personal Property**

41\. The parties accumulated household furnishings, appliances, electronics, artwork, and other personal effects during the course of the marriage. The majority of these items remain at the marital residence at 418 Birchwood Lane. Respondent estimates the combined fair market value of household contents and personal property at approximately Thirty Thousand Dollars (\$30,000.00).
